channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Toward General Semantic Chunking: A Discriminative Framework for Ultra-Long Documents
item.description: arXiv:2602.23370v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-document topic segmentation plays an important role in information retrieval and document understanding, yet existing methods still show clear shortcomings in ultra-long text settings. Traditional discriminative models are constrained by fixed wi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, release, experiment, retrieval, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Truncated Step-Level Sampling with Process Rewards for Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2602.23440v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Training large language models to reason with search engines via reinforcement learning is hindered by a fundamental credit assignment problem: existing methods such as Search-R1 provide only a sparse outcome reward after an entire multi-step trajecto...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, model, reasoning, experiment, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CiteAudit: You Cited It, But Did You Read It? A Benchmark for Verifying Scientific References in the LLM Era
item.description: arXiv:2602.23452v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific research relies on accurate citation for attribution and integrity, yet large language models (LLMs) introduce a new risk: fabricated references that appear plausible but correspond to no real publications. Such hallucinated citations have ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, paper, LLM, model, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FHIRPath-QA: Executable Question Answering over FHIR Electronic Health Records
item.description: arXiv:2602.23479v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Though patients are increasingly granted digital access to their electronic health records (EHRs), existing interfaces may not support precise, trustworthy answers to patient-specific questions. Large language models (LLM) show promise in clinical que...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, fine-tuning, reasoning, model, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: IDP Accelerator: Agentic Document Intelligence from Extraction to Compliance Validation
item.description: arXiv:2602.23481v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Understanding and extracting structured insights from unstructured documents remains a foundational challenge in industrial NLP. While Large Language Models (LLMs) enable zero-shot extraction, traditional pipelines often fail to handle multi-document ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, multimodal, reasoning, demonstration, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: France or Spain or Germany or France: A Neural Account of Non-Redundant Redundant Disjunctions
item.description: arXiv:2602.23547v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sentences like "She will go to France or Spain, or perhaps to Germany or France." appear formally redundant, yet become acceptable in contexts such as "Mary will go to a philosophy program in France or Spain, or a mathematics program in Germany or Fra...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, transformer, arxiv, large language model, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multi-Agent Causal Reasoning for Suicide Ideation Detection Through Online Conversations
item.description: arXiv:2602.23577v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Suicide remains a pressing global public health concern. While social media platforms offer opportunities for early risk detection through online conversation trees, existing approaches face two major limitations: (1) They rely on predefined rules (e....<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, experiment, platform, arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BRIDGE the Gap: Mitigating Bias Amplification in Automated Scoring of English Language Learners via Inter-group Data Augmentation
item.description: arXiv:2602.23580v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In the field of educational assessment, automated scoring systems increasingly rely on deep learning and large language models (LLMs). However, these systems face significant risks of bias amplification, where model prediction gaps between student gro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, model, experiment, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HumanMCP: A Human-Like Query Dataset for Evaluating MCP Tool Retrieval Performance
item.description: arXiv:2602.23367v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers contain a collection of thousands of open-source standardized tools, linking LLMs to external systems; however, existing datasets and benchmarks lack realistic, human-like user queries, remaining a critical gap in ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, LLM, model, retrieval, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: An Agentic LLM Framework for Adverse Media Screening in AML Compliance
item.description: arXiv:2602.23373v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Adverse media screening is a critical component of anti-money laundering (AML) and know-your-customer (KYC) compliance processes in financial institutions. Traditional approaches rely on keyword-based searches that generate high false-positive rates o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, model, retrieval, arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SleepLM: Natural-Language Intelligence for Human Sleep
item.description: arXiv:2602.23605v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present SleepLM, a family of sleep-language foundation models that enable human sleep alignment, interpretation, and interaction with natural language. Despite the critical role of sleep, learning-based sleep analysis systems operate in closed labe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, few-shot, analysis, cross-modal, multimodal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MMKG-RDS: Reasoning Data Synthesis via Deep Mining of Multimodal Knowledge Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2602.23632v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Synthesizing high-quality training data is crucial for enhancing domain models' reasoning abilities. Existing methods face limitations in long-tail knowledge coverage, effectiveness verification, and interpretability. Knowledge-graph-based approaches ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, reasoning, multimodal, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PseudoAct: Leveraging Pseudocode Synthesis for Flexible Planning and Action Control in Large Language Model Agents
item.description: arXiv:2602.23668v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents typically rely on reactive decision-making paradigms such as ReAct, selecting actions conditioned on growing execution histories. While effective for short tasks, these approaches often lead to redundant tool usage, u...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, LLM, model, reasoning, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ODAR: Principled Adaptive Routing for LLM Reasoning via Active Inference
item.description: arXiv:2602.23681v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The paradigm of large language model (LLM) reasoning is shifting from parameter scaling to test-time compute scaling, yet many existing approaches still rely on uniform brute-force sampling (for example, fixed best-of-N or self-consistency) that is co...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, model, reasoning, arxiv, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Detoxifying LLMs via Representation Erasure-Based Preference Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2602.23391v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) trained on webscale data can produce toxic outputs, raising concerns for safe deployment. Prior defenses, based on applications of DPO, NPO, and similar algorithms, reduce the likelihood of harmful continuations, but not r...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, LLM, fine-tuning, model, prompting | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: U-CAN: Utility-Aware Contrastive Attenuation for Efficient Unlearning in Generative Recommendation
item.description: arXiv:2602.23400v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative Recommendation (GenRec) typically leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to redefine personalization as an instruction-driven sequence generation task. However, fine-tuning on user logs inadvertently encodes sensitive attributes into model ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> instruction, LLM, fine-tuning, reasoning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Human Supervision as an Information Bottleneck: A Unified Theory of Error Floors in Human-Guided Learning
item.description: arXiv:2602.23446v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are trained primarily on human-generated data and feedback, yet they exhibit persistent errors arising from annotation noise, subjective preferences, and the limited expressive bandwidth of natural language. We argue that these l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> compression, model, experiment, retrieval, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Uncertainty-aware Language Guidance for Concept Bottleneck Models
item.description: arXiv:2602.23495v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs) provide inherent interpretability by first mapping input samples to high-level semantic concepts, followed by a combination of these concepts for the final classification. However, the annotation of human-understandabl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, LLM, model, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context window, context, prompt, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, retrieval, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, knowledge base, model, reasoning, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, platform, prompt, vector, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Causal Identification from Counterfactual Data: Completeness and Bounding Results
item.description: arXiv:2602.23541v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Previous work establishing completeness results for $\textit{counterfactual identification}$ has been circumscribed to the setting where the input data belongs to observational or interventional distributions (Layers 1 and 2 of Pearl's Causal Hierarch...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Global Interpretability via Automated Preprocessing: A Framework Inspired by Psychiatric Questionnaires
item.description: arXiv:2602.23459v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Psychiatric questionnaires are highly context sensitive and often only weakly predict subsequent symptom severity, which makes the prognostic relationship difficult to learn. Although flexible nonlinear models can improve predictive accuracy, their li...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, arxiv, context, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: Humans and LLMs Diverge on Probabilistic Inferences
item.description: arXiv:2602.23546v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human reasoning often involves working over limited information to arrive at probabilistic conclusions. In its simplest form, this involves making an inference that is not strictly entailed by a premise, but rather only likely given the premise. While...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, reasoning, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Planning under Distribution Shifts with Causal POMDPs
item.description: arXiv:2602.23545v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In the real world, planning is often challenged by distribution shifts. As such, a model of the environment obtained under one set of conditions may no longer remain valid as the distribution of states or the environment dynamics change, which in turn...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, vector, framework, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AI Must Embrace Specialization via Superhuman Adaptable Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2602.23643v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Everyone from AI executives and researchers to doomsayers, politicians, and activists is talking about Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). Yet, they often don't seem to agree on its exact definition. One common definition of AGI is an AI that can d...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, arxiv, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Brain-OF: An Omnifunctional Foundation Model for fMRI, EEG and MEG
item.description: arXiv:2602.23410v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Brain foundation models have achieved remarkable advances across a wide range of neuroscience tasks. However, most existing models are limited to a single functional modality, restricting their ability to exploit complementary spatiotemporal dynamics ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, model, multimodal, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EvoX: Meta-Evolution for Automated Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2602.23413v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work such as AlphaEvolve has shown that combining LLM-driven optimization with evolutionary search can effectively improve programs, prompts, and algorithms across domains. In this paradigm, previously evaluated solutions are reused to guide th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, prompt, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, tool, prompt, step-by-step, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Long Range Frequency Tuning for QML
item.description: arXiv:2602.23409v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantum machine learning models using angle encoding naturally represent truncated Fourier series, providing universal function approximation capabilities with sufficient circuit depth. For unary fixed-frequency encodings, circuit depth scales as O(om...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: FedDAG: Clustered Federated Learning via Global Data and Gradient Integration for Heterogeneous Environments
item.description: arXiv:2602.23504v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables a group of clients to collaboratively train a model without sharing individual data, but its performance drops when client data are heterogeneous. Clustered FL tackles this by grouping similar clients. However, existing...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
